task_id: ses_247787a45ffev2DKbjKjz4smDC (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim

  The fixed-shear state-locality / package-reduction route is the best next route because the draft has already compressed the residual mixed two-point burden to the exact fixed-shear corner, while separately ruling out quotient-only and single-determinant mechanisms as shear-blind. This makes fixed-shear not merely the strongest local theorem target but the narrowest route that attacks the actual remaining obstruction. It is also better than the abstract `\Qabs` route for clarifying the missing two-atom object, because the draft itself says the theorem-strength issue is no longer the ambient quotient architecture but verification of one of the two-point lanes for the actual corrected two-atom package. Success would likely identify the minimal provenance-sensitive state that survives quotient descent; failure would be equally informative, because it would show that even on the exact fixed-shear corner the obstruction is not state-local and must live in a stronger provenance-sensitive package layer.

- Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open | rejected`)

  heuristic

- Evidence

  The manuscript already says the residual mixed overlap burden is concentrated at the exact fixed-shear corner `\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}` and describes the remaining job as a three-front problem whose middle front is “exact fixed-shear quotient transport,” not another diffuse local classification problem. That is strong evidence that this route is globally prioritized by the current reduction, not just locally attractive.

  The draft also says quotient and single-determinant routes are exactly shear-blind on the mixed two-point branch. So any route that stays at the abstract quotient level, or only reads determinant/minor data, provably cannot see the repeated-node shear parameter that remains live on the bad branch. This is the main reason fixed-shear can be better than the abstract `\Qabs` route: it is aimed at the first place where the surviving obstruction is still visible.

  The paper further says the projective compactness theorem only gives a projectively frozen secant-shadow problem, not exact repeated-source transport for one fixed affine shear. So the missing theorem is precisely a state-locality / transport-closure upgrade from projective freezing to an exact fixed-shear package. That is the right interface between the already-compressed geometry and the still-missing package theorem.

  On the two-atom-object question, the draft explicitly distinguishes schematic uses of `\Qabs/\ell_h` from theorem-strength finite-order objects, and then says what remains architectural is verification of one of the two-point routes for the actual corrected two-atom package. That is a direct indication that the abstract quotient is no longer the main uncertainty. A fixed-shear closure theorem would force the manuscript to specify what the actual corrected two-atom package remembers at coincidence, under collision, and under transport; that is exactly the missing object.

  On hidden provenance-sensitive state, the draft repeatedly says the remaining order-7 and mixed-branch burden is provenance-sensitive, not merely quotient-septic, and asks for package statements such as same reduced image germ or collision-functoriality. A successful fixed-shear theorem would therefore identify which extra state is actually needed and show it is local enough to close the exact corner. A failure, if honest, would be even sharper: it would show that no state-local fixed-shear package suffices, so the hidden state must depend on more global provenance than the local collision/fixed-shear chart records.

- Exact refs

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12230-12255` for the statement that quotient and single-determinant routes are shear-blind.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12257-12334` for projective compactness of moving `\lambda`-shear defects and the conclusion that the branch reduces only to a projectively frozen secant-shadow problem.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12359-12383` for the statement that affine freezing is stronger than projective freezing and is not automatic.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12513-12560` for the explicit list of live downstream programs, including state-locality / transport closure / diagonal-collapse on the exact fixed-shear corner and a provenance-sensitive package theorem.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12562-12584` for the “current three-front compression” naming exact fixed-shear quotient transport as one of the three residual fronts.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:10797-10809` and `10840-10843` for the insistence that the remaining order-7 closure is provenance-sensitive, not merely quotient-septic.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11262-11307` for the statement that the rigorous finite-order object is known, while what remains is verification of one of the two-point routes for the actual corrected two-atom package.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:5649-5654` and `6560-6569` for the scope limitation of the abstract `\Qabs` layer and the note that finite-order identification does not globally settle later uses of `\Qabs`.

  `paper/findings.md:39-42` for the standing warning that non-quotient-visible hidden state remains unaudited.

  `paper/findings.md:64-75` for the recurring open gaps showing that the live unresolved issues are now bridge/package/factorization questions rather than new planar local geometry.

  `paper/unverified.tex:98-109` for UV-003, which already frames the residual exact fixed-shear lane as the current open reduction target.

- Dependencies

  A theorem-strength formulation of the actual corrected two-atom package on the mixed branch, not just schematic `\Qabs` language.

  One of the fixed-shear closure mechanisms named in the draft: state-locality, transport closure, or diagonal collapse at coincidence.

  A provenance-sensitive package statement strong enough to control coincidence/collision on the fixed-shear corner.

  If the edge-law route is used, a proved edge-law for the actual corrected defects plus control on the exceptional locus `M=0`.

- Strongest objection

  The strongest objection is that the fixed-shear route may still be one layer too low: the paper itself says projective freezing does not automatically upgrade to affine freezing, and it also says the honest remaining burden is provenance-sensitive package coincidence. So fixed-shear could turn out to be only a good diagnostic chart, not a closure theorem. In that case the real missing object may sit above any state-local fixed-shear transport law, and time spent there would mainly clarify failure modes rather than close the branch.

- Needed for promotion

  A proved theorem showing that on the exact fixed-shear corner the actual corrected two-atom package is determined by state-local data at coincidence, or else satisfies a transport-closure / diagonal-collapse law forcing coincidence with the one-atom package.

  A precise statement of what data the “actual corrected two-atom package” contains and how it descends from the current corrected block formalism.

task_id: ses_24775a958ffeLndy3mBUh1qIfF (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim  
A theorem stated only for the actual corrected defects \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\) on the residual involutive exact fixed-shear branch is mathematically coherent with the current draft, but only as a branch-local quotient-transport theorem, not as a free-standing replacement for the full corrected two-atom package. The draft already isolates this lane as the only residual mixed corner and explicitly allows a local theorem of “state-locality, transport closure, or a diagonal-collapse result for the actual corrected defect package after quotient-visible descent.” The smallest legitimate version would assume branch-local analyticity in the blow-up chart, swap-evenness, collision vanishing, and quotient-visible transport on the involutive branch, yielding
  \[
  E_{12}^{(3)}=\delta^2\,\mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,\delta^2),\qquad
  E_{12}^{(5)}=\delta^2\,\mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,\delta^2),
  \]
  with
  \[
  \mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,0)=c'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}c(m),\qquad
  \mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,0)=(A_5^{\mathfrak f})'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
  \]
  along that branch. This is a legitimate structural advance if it comes from quotient-visible branch data and closes the exact fixed-shear corner; if it is only an ad hoc branch identity with no locality/transport principle, it is mostly a local patch with weak transfer value.

- Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open | rejected`)  
heuristic

- Evidence  
The draft already separates the residual mixed burden into the exact fixed-shear corner \(\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}\) and says that corner is “quotient-diagonal / involutive” and should be attacked by a quotient-transport theorem, not by another global branch taxonomy. It also already proposes, in the blown-up collision chart \((m,\omega,\delta)\), a conditional edge law written only for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\), with the exact edge values above. That makes a local theorem using only these two corrected defects coherent in the draft’s notation and architecture.  
At the same time, the draft also warns that quotient and single-determinant routes are shear-blind on the mixed branch, so a local theorem cannot be justified by arbitrary quotient data alone; it needs the extra involutive fixed-shear hypothesis under which the branch is said to become quotient-visible. Hence the idea is structurally plausible, but not yet established.  
As for value: on good patches, the draft already shows that controlling \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\) is enough to force closeness/exclusion, so a true local theorem for the residual corner would close a named remaining lane. That is more than cosmetic. But without a transport/locality mechanism, it would not generalize to the later package-level coincidence/functoriality target and would remain a narrow patch.

- Exact refs  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11605-11623` defines \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\).  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11648-11702` gives exact identities showing these defects control the strengthened invariant coordinates.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11777-11826` shows \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\) alone already force defect-scale closeness on good \(M\neq0\) patches.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12149-12165` says the weaker quotient-diagonal route only needs a genuine corrected two-atom quotient map plus continuity and diagonal collapse.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12230-12255` says quotient and single-determinant routes are shear-blind on the mixed two-point branch.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12510` states the branch-local blow-up/edge-law target for the actual corrected defects and says the involutive exact fixed-shear branch should become quotient-visible.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12513-12560` lists the exact fixed-shear corner as a live downstream program and explicitly allows “state-locality, transport closure, or a diagonal-collapse result for the actual corrected defect package after quotient-visible descent.”  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12562-12583` compresses the remaining mixed burden to exact fixed-shear quotient transport.

- Dependencies  
A legitimate branch-local theorem would require:
  \[
  (m,\omega,\delta),\qquad \kappa=\frac{2\omega}{\delta},
  \]
  as the swap-compatible blow-up chart.  
Analytic branch-local germs for the actual corrected defects
  \[
  E_{12}^{(3)}(m,\omega,\delta),\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}(m,\omega,\delta).
  \]
Swap-evenness:
  \[
  E(m,-\omega,-\delta)=E(m,\omega,\delta).
  \]
Collision vanishing:
  \[
  E(m,0,0)=0.
  \]
Restriction to the residual branch
  \[
  \{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\},
  \]
  together with the draft’s claimed extra feature that on this involutive branch the state is quotient-visible.  
A branch-local transport parameter, in current notation naturally \(\kappa\) (or an equivalent scalar \(Q\) determining \(\kappa/2\)), so that the edge values are exactly
  \[
  c'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}c(m),\qquad (A_5^{\mathfrak f})'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
  \]
For exclusion on good patches, the nonvanishing input
  \[
  c(m)\neq 0,\qquad M(m)\neq 0,\qquad M:=c(A_5^{\mathfrak f})'-c'A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
  \]

- Strongest objection  
The draft does not currently prove that the involutive exact fixed-shear branch really carries enough quotient-visible state to determine the branch-local transport scalar and force the edge law for the actual corrected defects. In fact, the audit says quotient and determinant data are shear-blind on the mixed branch. So unless one proves that the involutive fixed-shear restriction genuinely removes that blindness, a theorem written only in terms of \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\) risks being an asserted branch ansatz rather than a consequence of established structure.

- Needed for promotion  
A proof that on the residual branch \(\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}\), the actual corrected defects \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\) are branch-locally defined analytic swap-even germs in \((m,\omega,\delta)\), vanish at \((\omega,\delta)=(0,0)\), and admit the quadratic/projective factorization with the stated edge values.  
A proof that the involutive branch is genuinely quotient-visible in the needed sense, i.e. that the branch transport scalar is determined by quotient data and is not blocked by the shear-blindness audit.  
If the goal is only local good-patch closure, it is enough to derive the edge law and then use \(M/c\neq0\).  
If the goal is broader transfer value, one also needs a locality/transport principle explaining why this branch-local theorem is canonical and how it interfaces with the later package-level coincidence/functoriality program.

Honest verdict: yes, a theorem only about \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\) on the involutive exact fixed-shear branch is coherent with the present draft, and in its best form it would be a real structural advance because it closes the named residual mixed corner. But in the current draft it is still only a heuristic target: the missing step is proving that the involutive branch really supplies the quotient-visible transport data needed to make such a theorem canonical rather than ad hoc.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_24775a865ffem9hVLF1AFUJ22Q (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Claim

Failure of the three current targets would separate the residual burden as follows.

1. If fixed-shear state-locality fails on the residual corner \(\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}\), then the actual corrected two-pair defect package is not determined by the descended quotient-visible transport state \(\mathcal T_N(t)\) alone. Since the draft already proves quotient-visible descent, rank-\(1\) transport on the quartic-sextic rung, and no finite hidden reset beyond \(t\leftrightarrow -t\), such a failure would identify the surviving obstruction as genuinely relational/provenance-sensitive two-atom data, or else non-finite-order dependence, rather than hidden local transport dynamics.

2. If direct evenness/diagonal vanishing fails for the actual cubic/quintic two-pair defects on the involutive fixed-shear branch, then the residual corner survives for a package-level reason already at the finite-order defect layer. Concretely: failure of evenness means the interaction defects detect atom-ordering under \(t\leftrightarrow -t\); failure of diagonal vanishing means coincident atoms still carry nonzero interaction defect. Either failure says the involutive Taylor-collapse mechanism is not the right closure principle for the actual package.

3. If quotient diagonal collapse of \(\mathcal F_2\) fails, then the one-pair quotient germ \(F_h\) is not the true diagonal limit of the corrected two-atom quotient object, so the weak quotient route to exact two-point exclusion fails already at quotient level. That would be evidence against naive coincidence/functoriality of two-atom \(\to\) one-atom quotient data, not just against a stronger source-level merger theorem.

4. The most informative failure for the broader package/functoriality problem is failure of quotient diagonal collapse of \(\mathcal F_2\). That failure would show the mismatch already at the lowest quotient-visible two-atom/one-atom interface, so any eventual package theorem would have to be genuinely provenance-sensitive and stronger than diagonal quotient functoriality. State-locality failure is weaker: it still leaves open a relational two-atom package theorem. Evenness/diagonal-vanishing failure is also informative, but only at the cubic/quintic defect layer rather than at the full quotient map.

Status

heuristic

Evidence

The draft explicitly narrows the residual mixed burden on the exact fixed-shear corner to “state-locality, transport closure, or a diagonal-collapse result” and separately to a “provenance-sensitive package theorem” for coincidence/functoriality. It then proves that finite quotient-visible transport data descend to \(u=t^2\), that the quartic-sextic transport state has local rank \(1\), and that there is no third local reset beyond involution. From that setup, the draft itself says any surviving obstruction must come from relational/provenance-sensitive two-atom structure or non-finite-order dependence, unless state-locality holds. It also isolates evenness plus diagonal vanishing as exactly the package-level inputs needed for the involutive \(O(t^2)\) collapse, and isolates continuity plus diagonal collapse of \(\mathcal F_2\) as the weaker quotient route. The comparative informativeness ranking is therefore an architectural reading of which interface fails first: transport-state reduction, finite-order package collapse, or full quotient coincidence.

Exact refs

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12543-12555`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12567-12583`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12042-12166`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21736-21758`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21956-22007`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22409-22505`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22523-22619`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22966-23096`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:24523-24543`
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:25011-25030`
`paper/findings.md:39-42`
`paper/findings.md:64-76`

Dependencies

Interpretation of the draft’s conditional architecture, especially the residual fixed-shear corner, the quotient-transport package, and the two-point quotient route. The ranking in item 4 depends on treating “broader package/functoriality” as the two-atom to one-atom coincidence problem highlighted in the remarks.

Strongest objection

These implications are mostly architectural rather than theorem-stated contrapositives. The draft proves what follows if certain hypotheses hold, and remarks on what would remain otherwise, but it does not formally prove that each failure has a unique interpretation; in particular, “failure” could mean falsehood of the target statement or merely failure of the current proof route.

Needed for promotion

A theorem-level trichotomy or contrapositive memo making the failure implications explicit:
1. state-locality fails \(\Rightarrow\) surviving obstruction is relational/provenance-sensitive or non-finite-order;
2. evenness/diagonal vanishing fails \(\Rightarrow\) package-level asymmetry or nonzero diagonal interaction is genuinely present;
3. \(\mathcal F_2\) diagonal collapse fails \(\Rightarrow\) quotient-level coincidence/functoriality already fails.
A clean comparison lemma ranking which failure is strongest for the package/functoriality program would also be needed.

Honest verdict: the draft already tells us fairly clearly what state-locality failure would mean, and fairly clearly what \(\mathcal F_2\) diagonal-collapse failure would mean. The evenness/diagonal-vanishing case is also readable, but less uniquely diagnostic because it sits at the finite-order defect layer rather than the full quotient map. The “most informative” judgment is persuasive, not proved.
</task_result>

- Claim

The reason is now sharper than before: on the quartic--sextic fixed-shear rung the draft proves that every finite quotient-visible transport state is already a function of the single scalar `Q`, so any still-live obstruction must add genuinely relational two-atom data. `\mathbf\Sigma` is exactly the strongest finite relational datum the draft currently isolates that is independent of the moving shears and of `\Lambda`. This makes the pair `(\mathbf\Sigma,Q)` the natural minimal candidate. But the draft still stops short of showing that the actual corrected two-pair defect package is state-local in `(\mathbf\Sigma,Q)`, or even that `\mathbf\Sigma` survives the quotient-visible descent in the needed functorial way.

  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:20853-21079` (`\mathbf\Sigma` is exact, shear-independent, and branch-determined)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12318-12333`, `12359-12383` (mixed branch reduces only to a projectively frozen secant-shadow problem; projective datum is the right downstream object)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12471-12476` (`Q` expected to control the quartic--sextic quadratic coefficient after quotient-visible descent)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22302-22335` (finite transport jets descend; canonical descended scalar `X` factors through `\widehat Q`)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22348-22378` (every finite quotient-visible transport state is a function of `Q` alone)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22380-22407` (no finite-order hidden reset inside that state)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22472-22499` (if the corrected two-pair package were state-local in finite transport state, interaction would collapse)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22507-22530` (any remaining obstruction is genuinely relational/provenance-sensitive two-atom input, not a second transport variable)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22579-22619` (`Q`-package does not yet prove the actual corrected two-pair defect package is state-local; remaining burden is relational/provenance-sensitive)  
  `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:10797-10809`, `12552-12555`, `12576-12583` (honest target is same reduced image germ / collision-functoriality, i.e. package-level coincidence, not just another scalar)

- Dependencies  
  This reading depends on restricting to the quartic--sextic residual fixed-shear corner, where the rank-one `Q` theorem is proved.  
  It also depends on treating `\mathbf\Sigma` as the best currently available finite relational datum on the mixed branch, even though the text does not explicitly descend `\mathbf\Sigma` through the involutive quotient variable `u=t^2`.  
  Finally it depends on interpreting “state-locality” and “provenance-sensitive package” as compatible with a finite package candidate rather than necessarily requiring infinite-jet or nonlocal data.

- Strongest objection  
  The draft itself leaves open exactly the step that `(\mathbf\Sigma,Q)` would need: there is no theorem that the corrected two-pair defect package factors through this pair. Worse, the secant-shadow geometry becomes delicate exactly at the residual corner `\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}`, while the draft’s honest theorem target is package-level coincidence such as reduced-image-germ equality or collision-functoriality. That suggests `\mathbf\Sigma` may record only a projection of the needed provenance data. So `(\mathbf\Sigma,Q)` is a plausible finite front end, but not yet plausible as the whole hidden package without an additional functoriality/state-locality theorem.

- Needed for promotion  
  A theorem that along the involutive fixed-shear branch the actual corrected two-pair defect package is locally determined by the descended transport state `Q` together with one relational shadow, ideally `\mathbf\Sigma`.  
  Or, slightly weaker, a proof that equality of `(\mathbf\Sigma,Q)` forces same reduced image germ or collision-functoriality.  
  Or a direct quotient-descent theorem showing that `\mathbf\Sigma` itself becomes a single-valued analytic quotient object on `u=t^2` and that the residual corrected defects are state-local in `(Q,\mathbf\Sigma)`.

Honest verdict: this is real progress. The draft now makes `Q` look exhausted as one-atom descended state, and that pushes the finite hidden-package guess into the concrete form “`Q` plus one relational datum.” Among current candidates, `\mathbf\Sigma` is the best fit for that second slot. But the text still points one level deeper: the likely true object is not merely the pair `(Q,\mathbf\Sigma)` as naked coordinates, but a provenance package for which `(Q,\mathbf\Sigma)` would be the visible finite coordinates if a state-locality/functoriality theorem can be proved.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_24923354bffeu0d3MC5DKcQAtA (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Material progress:

The best refinement of the finite hidden-package candidate is no longer
\[
\bigl(\widehat\Psi(h_1),\widehat\Psi(h_2),\Lambda\bigr)
\]
with raw shear \(\Lambda=-a_2/a_1\). The draft points to a more precise candidate:
\[
\bigl(\widehat\Psi(h_1),\widehat\Psi(h_2),[\delta c:\delta v_5:\delta u_5:\delta\lambda]\bigr),
\]
or in the blown-up close-collision chart, equivalently
\[
\bigl(\widehat\Psi(m),\text{first jet of }\widehat\Psi\text{ at }m,\kappa\bigr),
\qquad
\kappa=\frac{2\omega}{\delta},
\]
with
\[
\omega=\frac{\lambda-1}{\lambda+1},\qquad
m=\frac{h_1+h_2}{2},\qquad \delta=h_2-h_1.
\]

Why this is a real step further:

1. `\widehat\Psi` is confirmed as the right one-pair/projective datum for multi-pair extraction, not just a plausible guess.
Refs: `proof_of_rh.tex:11368-11513`, `12586-12610`, `24987-25001`.

2. Raw one-coordinate shear `\Lambda` is too naive.
The draft explicitly says quotient-only and single-determinant data are shear-blind, and exhibits
\[
d=\Lambda s_2-s_1,\qquad s_i=(c_i,u_{5,i},v_{5,i}),
\]
with wedge data independent of \(\Lambda\). So a package that only adds `\Lambda` to `\widehat\Psi(h_1),\widehat\Psi(h_2)` is still missing the actual branch-generated cancellation direction.
Refs: `12230-12255`.

3. The natural extra coordinate is projective, not affine.
On the mixed two-point branch, the automatic compactness is only for the projective defect direction
\[
[d_n]=[\delta_n(c):\delta_n(v_5):\delta_n(u_5):\delta_n(\lambda)],
\]
and the paper says this does not in general reduce to a fixed affine shear triple \((\eta_*,\xi_*,\gamma_*)\).
Refs: `12257-12334`, especially `12328-12333`; `12359-12383`.

4. In the close-collision regime, that projective direction collapses to one projective parameter.
The blown-up collision-cancellation chart gives
\[
\mathcal D_{h_1}-\lambda \mathcal D_{h_2}
=
-2\omega\,\mathcal D_m-\delta\,\mathcal D_m'+O((|\omega|+|\delta|)^2),
\]
so after blow-up the leading cancellation datum is
\[
\Theta_{m,\theta}=\cos\theta\,\mathcal D_m+\sin\theta\,\mathcal D_m',
\]
equivalently the scalar projective slope \(\kappa=2\omega/\delta\).
This is the strongest evidence so far that “two one-pair states plus one relational coordinate” could suffice locally near coincidence.
Refs: `12385-12445`, `12447-12511`.

5. This package is still not proved sufficient globally or exactly.
The paper’s own formulation of the remaining burden still asks for:
- actual defect regularity in the blown-up chart,
- quotient-diagonal closure/state-locality on the residual corner,
- a provenance-sensitive package theorem such as same reduced image germ or collision-functoriality.
That means the current evidence supports a refined finite candidate, but not closure.
Refs: `12513-12584`, `24985-25030`.

Most concrete upgraded conclusion:

The most plausible finite hidden package beyond `Q` is now:

\[
\text{visible scalar }Q
\quad+\quad
\text{two-point projective state carried by }
\widehat\Psi
\quad+\quad
\text{one blown-up cancellation direction } \kappa
\]
rather than `Q` plus `\widehat\Psi(h_1),\widehat\Psi(h_2)` plus raw `\Lambda`.

Clean theorem target suggested by the current text:

If the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects are analytic and swap-even in the blown-up chart \((m,\omega,\delta)\), then
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}=\delta^2\mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,\delta^2),\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}=\delta^2\mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,\delta^2),
\]
with \(\kappa=2\omega/\delta\).
That would be the first theorem genuinely supporting “finite hidden package = `\widehat\Psi` plus one relational coordinate.”
Refs: `12447-12511`.

Bottom line:
Yes, there is material evidence that `\widehat\Psi` plus one relational coordinate could suffice locally, but the one coordinate should be the blown-up projective cancellation direction `\kappa` or `[δc:δv_5:δu_5:δλ]`, not raw `\Lambda`. The strongest remaining objection is that the draft only gets projective freezing and local edge-law heuristics, not exact package functoriality.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_24923357dffeFC8YLwlci7mXis (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Material progress:

A branch-local theorem weaker than full `\kappa(Q)` descent does look sufficient for local closure.

What is enough:
If along the involutive residual branch `(h_1,h_2)=(h(t),h(-t))` one can prove
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=\delta(t)^2\,H_3\!\bigl(m(Q(t)),\kappa(t),\delta(t)^2\bigr),\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=\delta(t)^2\,H_5\!\bigl(m(Q(t)),\kappa(t),\delta(t)^2\bigr),
\]
with `H_3,H_5` locally bounded or analytic along the branch, then full `\kappa=\kappa(Q)` is not needed. Since
\[
\delta(t)=h(-t)-h(t)=-2h'(0)t+O(t^3),
\]
this already gives
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=O(t^2),\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=O(t^2),
\]
hence
\[
E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}=O((h_1-h_2)^2),
\]
and Proposition `residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner` closes the branch on `J\Subset\{c\neq 0,\ M\neq 0\}`.

Why `m(Q)` is enough branch-locally:
On the involutive branch, the midpoint
\[
m(t)=\frac{h(t)+h(-t)}{2}
\]
is even analytic in `t`, so it factors through `u=t^2`. On the quartic--sextic rung, `Q(t)=\widehat Q(t^2)` is a local analytic coordinate in `u`, and every finite descended transport state is already an analytic function of `Q` alone. So branch-locally one may absorb `m` into `Q` without needing any theorem that `m` is globally quotient-visible.

What this reduces the hidden burden to:
The true missing input is not `m(Q)`. It is regularity of the composed branch quantity
\[
t\mapsto H_j(m(Q(t)),\kappa(t),\delta(t)^2).
\]
So the minimal local theorem is:

1. `m` re-expresses branch-locally as `m(Q)`;
2. the actual defects admit a regular `\delta^2` factor along the involutive branch;
3. the residual factor stays bounded/analytic there.

That is strictly weaker than proving `\kappa=\kappa(Q)` or `\kappa=\kappa(\mathcal T_N)`.

Main obstruction that remains:
The paper only proves `\lambda\to1` as `\delta\to0` on the collision chart, not `\omega=O(\delta)`. So `\kappa=2\omega/\delta` may still fail to have a finite analytic branch value. If `\kappa(t)` blows up or oscillates and the composed `H_j(...)` is not regular, the `\delta^2` prefactor alone does not force closure. That is the precise place where a genuinely relational hidden datum could still survive.

Exact refs:
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12385-12445` for the collision chart and `\lambda\to1` as `\delta\to0`.
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12509` for the heuristic `\delta^2 H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)` edge-law target.
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21808-21833` for the involutive repeated-value branch.
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22226-22275` for quartic--sextic local injectivity of `Q`.
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22348-22407` for `\mathcal T_N=\mathbf F_N(Q)`.
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22966-23024` and `23072-23096` for the criterion that `O((h_1-h_2)^2)` / swap-evenness plus diagonal vanishing already closes the corner.
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11890-12040` for the stronger source-level model where symmetry plus diagonal collapse automatically produce `(h_1-h_2)^2` factorization without any `\kappa` descent at all.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_24771a81effexONqwQpe7iSSBn (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Claim

The current branch equations prove only collision-level convergence, not slope control: as a close exact mixed two-point branch collides, `\delta:=h_2-h_1\to 0` forces `\lambda\to 1`, hence `\omega=(\lambda-1)/(\lambda+1)\to 0`, and the first-order defect expansion is
`\mathcal D_{h_1}-\lambda\mathcal D_{h_2}=-2\omega\,\mathcal D_m-\delta\,\mathcal D_m'+O((|\omega|+|\delta|)^2)`.
Nothing currently proved gives `\omega=O(\delta)` or bounded `\kappa=2\omega/\delta` on the involutive exact fixed-shear branch. The draft explicitly says the automatic compactness is only projective, not affine, and affine `\lambda`-shears may diverge when `\delta_n(\lambda)` vanishes to higher order. The extra theorem that would force the local `\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)` route is a genuine collision-cancellation regularity theorem for the actual corrected defects in the blown-up chart, plus a branch theorem giving bounded `\kappa` (equivalently `\lambda-1=O(\delta)`) on the residual involutive fixed-shear lane. As written, bounded `\kappa` is a realistic heuristic intermediate target, but not a proved or even conditionally derived consequence of the current exact fixed-shear machinery.

Status

open

Evidence

What is proved now:
` \delta\to 0 => \lambda\to 1 ` on a compact good patch, because the cubic cancellation equation forces the weights to balance at collision; the draft then introduces `\omega=(\lambda-1)/(\lambda+1)`, so `\omega\to 0` follows immediately. The same remark gives only the mixed first-order expansion in `(m,\omega,\delta)`, not any estimate comparing `|\omega|` to `|\delta|`.

What is not proved now:
The projective compactness proposition reduces a close exact mixed branch only to convergence of projective defect directions `[ \delta_n(c):\delta_n(v_5):\delta_n(u_5):\delta_n(\lambda)]`. The next remark states explicitly that this is weaker than affine freezing and that the affine `\lambda`-shears can diverge if `\delta_n(\lambda)` vanishes faster than the other defect components. That is exactly the obstruction to deducing `\omega=O(\delta)`.

What the exact fixed-shear results do prove:
On the conditional exact fixed-shear `K_v=0` slice, the repeated-value locus is involutive: locally `(h_1,h_2)=(h(t),h(-t))`, and the quotient-visible transport scalar `Q=J_{u_5}/J_\lambda` descends to the even quotient variable `u=t^2`. In the quartic-sextic rung, `\widehat Q'(0)=\kappa_{2,2}\neq 0`, so quotient-side transport is locally rank-one and no hidden re-entry occurs after quotienting by the involution. But these results govern quotient transport in `t^2`; they do not identify `\omega` as an odd analytic function of `t` with the same order as `\delta`, and they do not bound `\omega/\delta`.

What extra theorem would suffice:
The draft itself proposes the right missing statement: if the actual corrected defect package `E(m,\omega,\delta)` is analytic in the swap-compatible collision chart, swap-even, and vanishes on the collision locus, then
`E(m,\omega,\delta)=\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)`.
To turn that identity into a usable local exclusion theorem along a branch, one still needs bounded `\kappa` on that branch, i.e. `\omega=O(\delta)` or `\lambda-1=O(\delta)`. Without that, `\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)` can run off to large `|\kappa|` and the `\delta^2` factor alone does not yield a uniform `O(\delta^2)` estimate.

Is bounded `\kappa` realistic:
Yes as a heuristic intermediate target, because `\kappa` is exactly the projective slope variable of the blow-up and the residual exact fixed-shear lane is already reduced to an involutive / quotient-diagonal problem. But the current text does not derive boundedness from the exact fixed-shear equations; it only isolates that lane and proves quotient-side parity and transport structure there.

Exact refs

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12257-12334`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12359-12383`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12385-12445`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12510`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12541-12559`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:20717-20851`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21766-21833`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21852-22008`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22226-22275`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22302-22470`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22966-23091`

Dependencies

The exact fixed-shear analysis after `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21766` is explicitly conditional on the “additional conditional exact fixed-shear overlap lane.” The proposed `\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)` factorization in `paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12510` is also stated as work in progress and assumes analyticity, swap-evenness, and collision vanishing for the actual corrected defect package.

Strongest objection

The current exact fixed-shear machinery may already bypass `\kappa` entirely: the later propositions show that if the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects are even under the involution and vanish on coincidence, then one gets `O((h_1-h_2)^2)` directly from the involutive parameter `t`, without any need to control `\omega/\delta`. So bounded `\kappa` may be a natural route for the collision-blow-up program, but it is not yet shown to be the indispensable route for closing the residual corner.

Needed for promotion

A promotable theorem would need all of:
1. A proved collision-cancellation regularity statement for the actual corrected defects in the `(m,\omega,\delta)` chart, with swap-evenness and diagonal vanishing.
2. A proved branch theorem on the residual involutive exact fixed-shear lane giving `\lambda-1=O(\delta)`, equivalently bounded `\omega/\delta`.
3. A uniform local bound on `\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)` for bounded `\kappa`, enough to deduce `E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}=O(\delta^2)`.
4. Combination with the existing defect-scale closeness estimate to exclude nontrivial close bad cores on `J\Subset\{c\neq 0,M\neq 0\}`.

Honest verdict: the draft currently proves only `\lambda\to 1` and `\omega\to 0` at collision, plus strong quotient-side involutive structure on the residual fixed-shear corner. It does not currently give any control on `\omega/\delta`, so bounded `\kappa` remains a plausible but still unproved intermediate target.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_24771a806ffeoU9YnvlUtTYb7u (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim  
A local theorem of the form
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=\delta(t)^2 H_3(t),\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=\delta(t)^2 H_5(t),
\qquad \delta(t):=h(t)-h(-t),
\]
with \(H_3,H_5\) merely locally bounded near \(t=0\) on the actual involutive branch, is already enough for local closure on any compact good patch \(J\Subset\{c\neq 0,\ M\neq 0\}\). The exact minimal statement is a branchwise quadratic divisibility estimate
\[
|E_{12}^{(3)}(t)|+\|E_{12}^{(5)}(t)\|\le C\,|\delta(t)|^2
\]
for small \(t\). This is strictly weaker than the draft’s proposed analytic edge-law
\[
E_{12}^{(j)}=\delta^2\,\mathcal H_j(m,\kappa,\delta^2),
\]
because it asks only for second-order vanishing along the involutive branch, not analytic control in the full blown-up chart nor edge values at \(\delta=0\). If proved, it would close the live exact fixed-shear residual corner on good patches, but it would still be a corner-patch theorem rather than a general collision-cancellation law.

- Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open`)  
open

- Evidence  
The closure implication itself is already in place. The draft proves on good patches that
\[
|h_1-h_2|\le C_J\bigl(|E_{12}^{(3)}|+\|E_{12}^{(5)}\|\bigr).
\]
So if one had
\[
|E_{12}^{(3)}|+\|E_{12}^{(5)}\|=O((h_1-h_2)^2),
\]
then
\[
|\delta|\le C'_J |\delta|^2,
\]
which is impossible for sufficiently small nonzero \(\delta\). Thus boundedness of the residual factor is enough, provided the factorization/estimate is along the actual branch and is uniform near the branch point. No analyticity of \(H_j\) is needed for that contradiction. In fact, even the weaker condition
\[
|E_{12}^{(3)}|+\|E_{12}^{(5)}\|=o(|h_1-h_2|)
\]
would suffice.

The current results closest to this are the two conditional closure propositions already in the paper. One proves closure from evenness plus vanishing at the involutive point, which yields \(O(t^2)\) directly. The other proves the same conclusion assuming the defects are analytic functionals of a finite quotient-visible transport state \(\mathcal T_N(t)\), using that \(\mathcal T_N(t)=\mathcal T_N(0)+O(t^2)\). These are stronger hypotheses than bounded residual factors, but they show the exact logical target: only second-order vanishing is needed.

The edge-law remark is stronger still: it seeks analytic blown-up-chart factorizations
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}=\delta^2\mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,\delta^2),\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}=\delta^2\mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,\delta^2),
\]
with explicit edge values. That would imply the branchwise bounded-factor statement, but the converse is far weaker and does not recover the projective parameter \(\kappa\) or any Weierstrass-style branch preparation away from the involutive branch.

So the smallest natural theorem to ask for is:

“Along every local exact residual fixed-shear involutive branch on a compact good patch, the actual corrected cubic and quintic defects satisfy
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}=(h_1-h_2)^2 B_3,\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}=(h_1-h_2)^2 B_5
\]
with \(B_3,B_5\) locally bounded near the coincidence point.”

Equivalently, using \(\delta(t)=2h'(0)t+O(t^3)\), one may ask only for
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=t^2\widetilde B_3(t),\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=t^2\widetilde B_5(t)
\]
with \(\widetilde B_j\) locally bounded. That is the exact theorem that would establish local closure.

As to plausibility: the route is plausible because the quotient/involution structure already forces the relevant descended transport quantities to be even analytic in \(t\) or analytic in \(u=t^2\). What is missing is not the contradiction step, but a theorem showing that the actual corrected two-pair defects inherit enough quotient-visible dependence or branchwise regularity. The present draft explicitly says this remains open.

- Exact refs  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11777-11827`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12510`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12543-12550`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22302-22345`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22348-22405`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22409-22470`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:22967-23070`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:25023-25030`

- Dependencies  
A proof of branchwise quadratic divisibility for the actual corrected defects on the exact fixed-shear involutive branch. Sufficient forms include:
\[
|E_{12}^{(3)}|+\|E_{12}^{(5)}\|\le C|h_1-h_2|^2,
\]
or equivalently exact factorizations by \((h_1-h_2)^2\) with locally bounded residuals. A stronger sufficient package is quotient-visible/state-local dependence of the defects on the descended transport state, as in the conditional proposition. Full blown-up-chart analyticity is not logically required for local closure.

- Strongest objection  
This route only closes the already-isolated exact fixed-shear involutive corner on good \(M\neq 0\) patches. It does not by itself give the full collision-cancellation edge law, does not control the exceptional locus \(M=0\), and does not prove that the actual corrected defects are quotient-visible or even branch-regular enough for the bounded-factor statement. So success here may be mathematically real but still narrowly local.

- Needed for promotion  
A theorem for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects, not a model package, proving branchwise second-order vanishing on the involutive branch. The cleanest promotable statement is:
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}=(h_1-h_2)^2 B_3,\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}=(h_1-h_2)^2 B_5,
\]
with \(B_3,B_5\) locally bounded near coincidence on each compact good patch. Alternatively, prove the stronger state-local hypothesis of `prop:conditional-state-local-defect-closure-fixed-shear-corner` for the actual defects.

Honest verdict: bounded residual factor is enough for local closure, and the exact theorem needed is much smaller than full edge-law analyticity. But the draft does not currently prove that theorem for the actual corrected defects, and if obtained it would mostly close the live corner rather than deliver a broad new global package.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_2476f8dccffeYcd8it7j5AzULF (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim  
The cleanest plausible route is: use the proved one-pair swap law to show that the actual corrected two-pair cubic and quintic defects, written in the collision-cancellation chart \((m,\omega,\delta)\), are analytic, swap-even, and zero on the collision locus; this forces quadratic factorization \(E_{12}^{(3)}=\delta^2\mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,\delta^2)\), \(E_{12}^{(5)}=\delta^2\mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,\delta^2)\) with \(\kappa=2\omega/\delta\). On the involutive fixed-shear branch, where the problem is already reduced to a quotient-diagonal transport problem, swap invariance should then follow because the branch is parametrized by the swap-fixed projective shear variable and the edge data are exactly the one-pair quantities \(c\) and \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\).

- Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open | rejected`)  
heuristic

- Evidence  
The strongest already-proved one-pair symmetry is the raw swap law from symmetric placement, \(\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(-s;m)=J_0\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(s;m)J_0\), which is what makes the odd one-pair germ genuinely odd under the point-swap involution. After cubic normalization this becomes \(R_h(-z)=J_0R_h(z)J_0\), forcing the raw quintic slice and the realized quintic gauge shadows to be anti-fixed; hence the first residual ambiguity is entirely anti-fixed/traceless. The fixed-sector package then isolates the swap-fixed data \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\) and the quotient-septic detector \(\Delta_7\), while pure same-point septic terms are shown to be even under swap and therefore invisible in the odd germ.  

For two pairs, the draft itself says the remaining exact source-level input is just three identities: swap symmetry, one-amplitude collapse, and diagonal merger. It also identifies the residual mixed corner as the exact fixed-shear corner \(\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}\), already reduced to a quotient-diagonal / involutive transport problem. In the blown-up chart it further records the expected parity mechanism: if the actual corrected defect is analytic, swap-even, and vanishes at collision, then linear terms disappear and the defect factors as \(\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)\). The draft then states the specific desired edge laws
\[
\mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,0)=c'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}c(m),\qquad
\mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,0)=(A_5^{\mathfrak f})'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\]
which is exactly the first bridge from one-pair symmetry to two-pair corrected defects.  

So the relevant proved one-pair symmetries are:
`1.` symmetric-placement swap covariance of the corrected one-pair block;  
`2.` anti-fixedness of raw quintic and quintic gauge ambiguity under \(A\mapsto J_0AJ_0\);  
`3.` fixed/anti-fixed splitting of higher coefficients, especially the fixed-sector package \(A_5^{\mathfrak f},\Delta_7\);  
`4.` evenness of pure same-point septic terms under swap, hence their disappearance from the odd germ.  

These plausibly lift to the actual two-pair defects by packaging the corrected two-pair outputs in the swap-compatible chart \((m,\omega,\delta)\), proving the package is invariant under \((m,\omega,\delta)\mapsto(m,-\omega,-\delta)\), and then passing to the quotient-visible exact fixed-shear branch where only the projective shear parameter survives.

- Exact refs  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:2311-2317` for the basic corrected one-pair swap law.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:6625-6647` for cubic-normalized swap-symmetric raw corrected germs.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:6650-6698` for anti-fixedness of the raw quintic slice.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:6793-6836` for anti-fixedness of quintic gauge shadows.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:6838-6881` for trace-free anti-fixed quintic ambiguity.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:6955-7031` for the fixed/anti-fixed decomposition and \(A_5^{\mathfrak f},\Delta_7\).  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:7065-7105` for the fixed-sector septic gauge law.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:7778-7801` for swap-even pure same-point septic terms vanishing in the odd germ.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11996-12039` for the conditional quadratic defect estimate from a source-level realization.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12168-12189` for the exact stated missing inputs: swap symmetry, one-amplitude collapse, diagonal merger.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12385-12445` for the swap-compatible collision-cancellation chart \((m,\omega,\delta)\).  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12510` for the explicit expected quadratic/projective factorization and edge laws for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects.  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12533-12550` and `12570-12583` for the claim that the residual mixed corner is quotient-diagonal / involutive transport.

- Dependencies  
A proof of analytic state-locality for the actual corrected two-pair defect package in the blown-up chart.  
A proof that the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects are swap-even under \((m,\omega,\delta)\mapsto(m,-\omega,-\delta)\).  
A proof of one-amplitude collapse and diagonal merger for the actual corrected package.  
A descent statement showing that on the exact fixed-shear branch the defect depends only on quotient-diagonal data, not on hidden source provenance.  
Compatibility between the one-pair fixed-sector package \((c,A_5^{\mathfrak f})\) and the two-pair corrected transport object used to define the actual defects.

- Strongest objection  
There is a real failure mode: the naive source-summed whitened model is even in the amplitude and therefore cannot supply the required one-atom linearity or coincident-atom additivity. That means swap invariance is not automatic from the raw corrected coefficients themselves; it must come from a different lifted transport object. A second obstruction is that quotient and single-determinant routes are shear-blind, so if the actual corrected two-pair package retains source-level provenance or shear information not seen by the quotient-diagonal variables, then apparent involutivity at the quotient level may be too weak to force genuine swap invariance of the actual defects.

- Needed for promotion  
State and prove a lemma of the form: for the actual corrected two-pair cubic and quintic defects \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\), the blown-up chart realization \(E(m,\omega,\delta)\) is analytic, satisfies \(E(m,-\omega,-\delta)=E(m,\omega,\delta)\), and vanishes at \((\omega,\delta)=(0,0)\).  
Derive from that lemma the quadratic/projective factorization \(E=\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)\).  
Prove the edge identities \(\mathcal H_3(m,\kappa,0)=c'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}c(m)\) and \(\mathcal H_5(m,\kappa,0)=(A_5^{\mathfrak f})'(m)-\frac{\kappa}{2}A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\).  
Prove that along the exact fixed-shear branch the actual corrected defect descends to quotient-diagonal data, so the induced branch law is swap-invariant.  
Then insert this into the source-level merger criterion at `12168-12189`.

Honest verdict: the proof path is coherent and fairly tight, and the first nontrivial bridge is not a new algebraic identity but a regularity-plus-symmetry lemma for the actual corrected two-pair defects in the blown-up swap chart. Swap invariance is plausible, but not yet secure, because the draft has not proved that the actual corrected transport object both respects the swap chart and forgets hidden source/shear provenance on the exact fixed-shear branch.

task_id: ses_2476f8d72ffeW7MVhV2XGr55kC (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim  
The cleanest plausible proof path for vanishing of the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects on coincident atoms along the involutive residual branch is the `state-locality at the coincident state` route. It is closest to the current paper objects because the draft already descends the residual fixed-shear corner to a rank-one quotient-visible transport state `Q`, proves no finite-order hidden reset beyond `t \leftrightarrow -t`, and then shows that if `E_{12}^{(3)}` and `E_{12}^{(5)}` are analytic functionals of that descended state and vanish at coincidence, the residual branch is excluded. The exact minimal theorem to attack first is a package-level coincidence theorem: along the involutive branch `(h_1,h_2)=(h(t),h(-t))`, the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects are swap-even and vanish at `t=0` (equivalently on coincident atoms). The one-amplitude-collapse plus diagonal-merger route is stronger but farther from the current corrected objects, and `same reduced image germ at coincidence` is better read as the next provenance-sensitive theorem after cubic/quintic closure, mainly for the order-7 package.

- Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open`)  
heuristic

- Evidence  
1. Best conceptual route: `state-locality at the coincident state`. The draft already packages the residual corner `\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}` as quotient-diagonal / involutive, with repeated values given only by `t` and `-t`, and with the quartic-sextic rung reduced to one local scalar `Q`; this is exactly the setting where coincidence vanishing plus swap-evenness kills linear terms and forces `O((h_1-h_2)^2)` defects.  
2. Closest route to current paper objects: again `state-locality`. The paper already has the chain `Q -> transport jets \Theta_k -> finite state \mathcal T_N -> conditional defect closure`. By contrast, the source-level merger route still needs an actual corrected two-atom package satisfying one-amplitude collapse and diagonal merger, and the draft explicitly says the naive source-summed corrected-block route cannot provide that linearity because the extracted coefficients are even in the source weight.  
3. Minimal theorem to attack first: prove the package-level statement isolated in the residual-corner reduction, namely that the actual corrected cubic and quintic two-pair defects are invariant under the involution `t \leftrightarrow -t` and vanish on coincident atoms. A slightly stronger but structurally natural version is: there exists some finite descended transport state `\mathcal T_N` and analytic functionals `\mathfrak e_3,\mathfrak e_5` with `E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=\mathfrak e_3(\mathcal T_N(t))`, `E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=\mathfrak e_5(\mathcal T_N(t))`, and both zero at `t=0`.  
4. What this still leaves open: it does not settle the provenance-sensitive order-7 issue. The draft still identifies the honest remaining higher-order burden as `same reduced image germ at coincidence` or `collision-functoriality` strong enough to force quotient-septic collapse. It also leaves open the possibility that any surviving obstruction is genuinely relational or non-finite-order rather than encoded in the descended transport state.

- Exact refs  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex`:
`11888-11931` (`\label{lem:minimal-source-level-two-point-criterion}`), source-level stronger route: swap symmetry + one-amplitude collapse + diagonal merger imply quadratic factorization.  
`12168-12189` (`\label{rem:wip-source-level-reduction-two-point-branch}`), explicit statement that these are the exact remaining source-level inputs.  
`12192-12227` (`\label{rem:audit-even-amplitude-obstruction-naive-source-summed-two-point}`), obstruction to deriving one-amplitude linearity from the naive corrected-block package.  
`12385-12510` (`\label{rem:wip-collision-cancellation-chart-two-point}`, `\label{rem:wip-parity-projective-factorization-collision-blow-up}`), swap-compatible collision chart and the quadratic/projective edge-law heuristic for actual corrected cubic/quintic defects.  
`12537-12555`, `12562-12584`, residual corner identified as quotient-diagonal / involutive, with three downstream fronts and provenance-sensitive package theorem deferred.  
`21740-21763`, summary that on the residual corner the finite transport state has local rank `1`, and if actual corrected cubic/quintic defects are state-local then the corner is already excluded.  
`21808-21833` (`\label{lem:local-exact-repeated-value-locus-involutive-fixed-shear}`), only local repeated-value partner is `t \leftrightarrow -t`.  
`21852-21944` (`\label{prop:quotient-transport-scalar-residual-ladder-fixed-shear-Kv-zero}`), even quotient transport scalar `Q(t)=\widehat Q(t^2)` and residual ladder.  
`22226-22255` (`\label{cor:quartic-sextic-quotient-injectivity-local-no-reentry}`), first residual rung is quotient-injective downstairs.  
`22302-22346` (`\label{prop:finite-transport-jet-quotient-descent-fixed-shear-corner}`), every finite transport jet descends analytically to `u=t^2`.  
`22348-22407` (`\label{cor:rank-one-transport-state-quartic-sextic-fixed-shear}`, `\label{cor:no-finite-order-hidden-reset-quartic-sextic-fixed-shear}`), finite quotient-visible transport state is locally a function of `Q` alone and has no hidden reset.  
`22409-22470` (`\label{prop:conditional-state-local-defect-closure-fixed-shear-corner}`), conditional state-local defect closure.  
`22472-22500` (`\label{cor:stronger-state-local-package-collapse-fixed-shear-corner}`), stronger merger law gives identically zero interaction remainder along the involutive branch.  
`22966-23024` (`\label{prop:residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner}`), evenness + vanishing at coincidence imply `O((h_1-h_2)^2)` and exclusion.  
`23072-23096` (`\label{cor:package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner}`), minimal package-level target: swap invariance plus vanishing on coincident atoms.  
`10794-10809`, `21321-21329`, order-7 burden framed as `same reduced image germ at coincidence` or `collision-functoriality`.

- Dependencies  
The residual fixed-shear overlap lane and involutive parametrization near `a \in \{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}`; quotient-visible descent to `Q(t)=\widehat Q(t^2)`; local injectivity of `\widehat Q` on the quartic-sextic rung; swap symmetry of the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects; vanishing of those defects on coincident atoms; for the stronger route, an actual corrected two-atom package satisfying one-amplitude collapse and diagonal merger.

- Strongest objection  
The paper does not yet derive `state-locality` or even package-level diagonal vanishing for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects from the corrected block formulas themselves. The present state-local route is structurally well aligned, but it still assumes exactly the missing theorem: that the actual corrected defect package depends only on the descended transport state at coincidence, rather than on additional genuinely relational provenance data.

- Needed for promotion  
Prove `\label{cor:package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner}` for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects, preferably in the stronger form of `\label{prop:conditional-state-local-defect-closure-fixed-shear-corner}`: construct a finite descended transport state `\mathcal T_N`, show `E_{12}^{(3)}` and `E_{12}^{(5)}` are analytic functionals of `\mathcal T_N`, and verify they vanish at `t=0`. After that, attack the provenance-sensitive next theorem: `same reduced image germ at coincidence` or `collision-functoriality`, to force the quotient-septic collapse and close the honest order-7 remainder.

Honest verdict: the best first attack is not one-amplitude collapse. It is the residual-corner package theorem “swap-even + zero on coincidence,” ideally upgraded to state-locality in the descended rank-one transport state. That is the route most native to the current paper. If it works, cubic/quintic diagonal vanishing becomes enough to kill the involutive residual branch on good patches. What remains afterward is the provenance-sensitive order-7 package, not the cubic/quintic branch itself.
</task_result>

task_id: ses_2476f8c91ffej9uh0Xs0bKY4Gc (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim  
The smallest realistic packet is a 3-lemma packet.

1. `Lemma A (swap descent/invariance)`: along the residual exact fixed-shear involutive branch \((h_1,h_2)=(h(t),h(-t))\), the actual corrected defects satisfy
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=E_{12}^{(3)}(-t),\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=E_{12}^{(5)}(-t).
\]
This feeds directly into Proposition `prop:residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner` and Corollary `cor:package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner`.

2. `Lemma B (coincidence vanishing / diagonal merger)`: when the two atoms coincide, the actual corrected defects vanish:
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}=0,\qquad E_{12}^{(5)}=0\quad\text{on }h_1=h_2.
\]
This also feeds directly into Proposition `prop:residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner` and Corollary `cor:package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner`; in package form it is the diagonal-merger input isolated abstractly by Lemma `lem:minimal-source-level-two-point-criterion`.

3. `Lemma C (chart regularity / branch restriction)`: the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects admit analytic restriction to the swap-compatible collision/cancellation chart near the involutive branch, so Lemmas A and B are statements about genuine analytic germs rather than only formal identities. This feeds into the draft’s intended edge-law theorem for the actual corrected defects and supplies the analytic framework needed before A and B can be applied cleanly on the residual corner.

Likely hardest: `Lemma B`, because the draft already audits that the naive source-summed corrected-block model cannot prove one-atom linearity or coincident-atom additivity.  
Likely easiest and best attacked first: `Lemma A`, because the residual branch has already been reduced to the involution \(t\leftrightarrow -t\) with no third local partner, so swap symmetry is the most formal part of theorem C.

- Status  
open

- Evidence  
The draft already proves the reduction step: Proposition `prop:residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner` needs only evenness and vanishing at \(t=0\), and Corollary `cor:package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner` says it is enough to verify swap invariance and coincidence vanishing for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects. The surrounding roadmap then identifies exactly what is still missing: a good-patch edge-law theorem for the actual corrected defects, a quotient-visible closure on the residual corner, and a provenance-sensitive coincidence/functoriality theorem. The abstract source-level factorization lemma shows why swap symmetry plus one-amplitude collapse plus diagonal merger would be enough, while the audit shows the naive model does not supply the diagonal-merger input. That makes the realistic packet “formal symmetry first, merger second, analytic chart regularity around both.”

- Exact refs  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:11888-12009`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12192-12227`  
`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12452-12510`

<task_result>
Claim

There is a concrete lift chain from the proved one-pair swap laws to the desired swap invariance of the actual corrected cubic/quintic two-pair defects, but one genuinely unproved bridge remains.

The chain is:

1. One-pair corrected swap law at the block level:
\[
\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(-s;m)=J_0\,\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(s;m)\,J_0,
\]
and the extracted odd scalar changes sign under this conjugation. This is the basic proved one-pair swap law.

2. After cubic normalization, the one-pair raw corrected germ satisfies
\[
R_h(-z)=J_0R_h(z)J_0,
\]
so the cubic and quintic coefficients transform by the induced involution, and the quintic ambiguity space is anti-fixed/traceless.

3. On the residual exact fixed-shear corner, the repeated-value branch is already proved to be involutive:
\[
(h_1,h_2)=(h(t),h(-t)),
\]
with no third local partner beyond \(t\leftrightarrow -t\).

4. Therefore, if the actual corrected two-pair cubic/quintic defect extraction is swap-equivariant with respect to exchanging the two atoms, then along the involutive branch one gets
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(t)=E_{12}^{(3)}(-t),\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=E_{12}^{(5)}(-t).
\]

The first genuinely unproved step is exactly step 4: lifting the proved one-pair swap/conjugation law to the actual corrected two-pair defect package after all normalization, quotient, and interaction subtraction.

A compact lemma that isolates this without overreaching is:

“Swap-equivariant defect descent lemma.”
Along any local exact fixed-shear involutive branch \((h_1,h_2)=(h(t),h(-t))\), the actual corrected cubic and quintic interaction defects are analytic functionals of the unordered two-atom corrected package, hence are invariant under atom exchange:
\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(h_1,h_2)=E_{12}^{(3)}(h_2,h_1),\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}(h_1,h_2)=E_{12}^{(5)}(h_2,h_1).
\]

If that lemma were proved, then swap invariance itself would become formal on the involutive branch. Together with the separate coincidence-vanishing input already isolated in the draft, Proposition~\ref{prop:residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner} would apply immediately.

Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open | rejected`)

heuristic

Evidence

The plausibility is strong because the paper already proves the exact ingredients on the one-pair side and on the branch-geometry side, and the missing bridge is narrow.

The one-pair side already has:
\[
\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(-s;m)=J_0\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(s;m)J_0,
\]
and later, after cubic normalization,
\[
R_h(-z)=J_0R_h(z)J_0.
\]
So the corrected one-pair package is already organized around the same swap involution.

The branch side already has:
\[
\Phi(t)=(t^m,G(t)),\qquad m\ \text{even},\qquad G(-t)=G(t),
\]
and hence the exact repeated-value locus is exactly \(t_2=-t_1\). So on the residual branch, replacing \(t\) by \(-t\) is literally the atom swap.

What is not proved is that the actual corrected two-pair defect extractor preserves this symmetry after passing from raw one-pair corrected objects to the interaction defects \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\). The draft itself identifies this as the remaining package-level issue.

Exact refs

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex`
Lines 2294-2318: one-pair corrected oddness from symmetric placement and jet-basis swap symmetry.
Lines 6597-6622: definition of the swap involution on cubic/quintic germs.

swap-equivariance of the actual corrected two-pair package under exchanging the two atoms;

for branch exclusion beyond mere swap invariance, also diagonal/coincidence vanishing.

The proved swap law is a one-pair law in the microscopic separation variable \(s\), while the desired conclusion concerns actual corrected two-pair interaction defects after whitening, normalization, quotient descent, and subtraction of one-pair contributions. The paper does not yet prove that these operations commute with atom exchange in the required functorial way. So the argument is not blocked by branch geometry anymore; it is blocked by package-equivariance.

\[
E_{12}^{(3)}(h_1,h_2)=E_{12}^{(3)}(h_2,h_1),\qquad
E_{12}^{(5)}(h_1,h_2)=E_{12}^{(5)}(h_2,h_1)
\]
for the actual corrected defects.

Honest verdict:

task_id: ses_2476d4e52ffeSY1qiqkiYXjHqY (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Claim

The smallest reasonable regularity target is not full three-variable analyticity of the entire corrected package in \((m,\omega,\delta)\). What is minimally needed before swap invariance and diagonal vanishing become mathematically useful is:

1. A local involutive parametrization of the actual exact fixed-shear branch by a centered source variable \(t\), with partner map \(t\leftrightarrow -t\).
2. Branchwise continuity up to \(t=0\) so the diagonal value is defined and vanishing is meaningful.
3. Branchwise analyticity in the descended quotient variable \(u=t^2\) for the actual corrected cubic and quintic defects.

Under that lemma, each defect component has the form
\[
E^{(j)}(t)=\widetilde E^{(j)}(u),\qquad u=t^2,\qquad \widetilde E^{(j)}(0)=0,
\]
hence
\[
E^{(j)}(t)=u\,\widetilde H^{(j)}(u)=t^2\widetilde H^{(j)}(t^2).
\]
If along the same branch \(\delta(t)\) is an odd local coordinate with a simple zero, this becomes exactly the desired blown-up form
\[
E^{(j)}(m,\omega,\delta)=\delta^2 H_j(m,\kappa,\delta^2).
\]

So:

1. The absolute minimum for “meaningful” swap invariance plus diagonal vanishing is continuity plus a well-defined involution, but that is only enough for the weak diagonal-collapse route, not for the quadratic/projective edge law.
2. Full analyticity in all of \((m,\omega,\delta)\) is stronger than necessary; branchwise analyticity after descent to the involutive quotient \(u=t^2\) should suffice for the local packet. \(C^1\) is not enough to force the quadratic factorization.
3. The strongest defensible regularity level suggested by the current draft is analytic or real-analytic branchwise regularity, repeatedly after centered reparametrization and quotient descent.
4. The exact minimal lemma auto-run should attack is:

“On the exact fixed-shear \(K_v=0\) corner, after shrinking and choosing a centered involutive source coordinate \(t\), the actual corrected cubic and quintic defects descend to analytic germs of \(u=t^2\) and vanish at \(u=0\). Equivalently, along each local involutive branch they are even analytic germs with zero constant term.”

Status

heuristic

Evidence

The draft already separates two levels of needed regularity. For the weak exact two-point exclusion route, it asks only continuity of the actual corrected quotient map together with exact diagonal collapse; this is enough to pass to the limit on coalescing bad cores, but it does not produce any quadratic factorization. By contrast, the collision-cancellation remark explicitly assumes analyticity in the swap-compatible chart to derive the form \(E=\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)\). Later exact fixed-shear remarks sharpen the geometry further: after centered analytic reparametrization, the residual branch is involutive, transport jets descend analytically to \(u=t^2\), and the finite quotient-visible transport state is locally an analytic function of a single scalar \(Q\). That makes branchwise analytic descent to the quotient variable the smallest lemma matching the actual structure already isolated by the paper.

Exact refs

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12042-12165` for the weaker continuity-plus-diagonal-collapse route.

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12447-12510` for the explicit analytic hypothesis in \((m,\omega,\delta)\) and the target factorization
\[
E(m,\omega,\delta)=\delta^2\mathcal H(m,\kappa,\delta^2).
\]

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:12543-12550` for the statement that what remains is “enough regularity and swap-evenness” for the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects, and then a separate quotient-diagonal closure theorem.

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21740-21763` for analytic descent of finite transport jets to the quotient variable and analytic state-locality in the exact fixed-shear corner.

`paper/proof_of_rh.tex:21766-21850` for the involutive centered parametrization \(t\leftrightarrow -t\), even/even quotient germ structure, and the exact repeated-value locus.

Dependencies

A proved local involutive parametrization of the exact fixed-shear branch.

A proof that the actual corrected cubic/quintic defects are genuine branchwise functions on that branch, not provenance-sensitive two-atom data.

Analytic descent of those actual defects to the quotient variable \(u=t^2\), or an equivalent branchwise Weierstrass/preparation statement.

If one wants the literal \(\delta^2 H(m,\kappa,\delta^2)\) form rather than just \(t^2\widetilde H(t^2)\), a proof that \(\delta(t)\) is an odd local coordinate with simple vanishing on the branch.

Strongest objection

The paper currently proves analytic quotient descent and rank-one state-locality only for finite transport jets / quotient-visible transport states, not for the actual corrected defect package itself. The draft explicitly warns that any surviving obstruction may come from genuinely relational or provenance-sensitive two-atom data beyond the descended transport state. If that happens, even branchwise analyticity in \(u=t^2\) for the actual defects may fail, so the proposed minimal lemma could still be too optimistic.

Needed for promotion

A theorem of the following form:

“Let \(E^{(3)},E^{(5)}\) denote the actual corrected cubic and quintic defects on the exact fixed-shear \(K_v=0\) slice. After shrinking near a centered coincidence point, there is a real-analytic local parameter \(t\) such that the repeated-value involution is \(t\mapsto -t\), and there exist analytic germs \(\widetilde E_3,\widetilde E_5\) with
\[
E^{(j)}(t)=\widetilde E_j(t^2),\qquad \widetilde E_j(0)=0,\qquad j=3,5.
\]
”

If additionally \(\delta(t)\) is shown to be an odd simple coordinate, record the corollary
\[
E^{(j)}=\delta^2 H_j(m,\kappa,\delta^2).
\]

Honest verdict: continuity is enough only for the weak diagonal-collapse route; \(C^1\) does not buy the quadratic/projective package; the strongest level actually supported by the paper’s current architecture is branchwise analytic regularity after involutive quotient descent, and that is the minimal lemma worth attacking.
</task_result>

Claim

Assuming swap invariance of the actual corrected cubic and quintic defects along the involutive branch, the local exact fixed-shear corner reduces to one remaining package-level input: diagonal vanishing on coincidence. open

Evidence

The draft now states the package-level reduction explicitly. Proposition `residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner` shows that if the cubic and quintic defects are even under the involutive swap and vanish at the coincidence point, then they are \(O(t^2)\), hence \(O((h_1-h_2)^2)\), and the residual exact fixed-shear involutive bad branch is excluded on compact good patches. Corollary `package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner` then says it is enough to verify exactly two inputs for the actual corrected defects: swap invariance along \(t \leftrightarrow -t\), and vanishing on coincident atoms. So, under your hypothesis that swap invariance is established, diagonal vanishing is the only remaining local input for corner closure in that package-level route.

The cleanest next theorem is therefore not another transport or branch-taxonomy theorem, but a direct diagonal-vanishing theorem on the actual corrected defect package at coincidence. The surrounding remarks sharpen this further: the corner is already quotient-diagonal/involutive; finite transport jets descend to the quotient; on the quartic-sextic rung the quotient-visible transport state has local rank one; and there is no third finite-order reset beyond the involution. The draft then says what is not yet proved is that the actual corrected two-pair defect package is state-local in that descended transport state. That makes the minimal next theorem the diagonal-vanishing/package-collapse statement, with state-locality as a stronger optional route.

Dependencies

The reduction uses the additional conditional exact fixed-shear overlap lane, the involutive parametrization of the repeated-value branch, and the good-patch defect-scale estimate invoked through `cor:bad-two-pair-cores-defect-scale-close`. The stronger optional route also depends on state-locality of the actual corrected two-pair package in the descended transport state and, for full package collapse, the natural merger law.

Strongest objection

“Diagonal vanishing is the only real missing input locally” is correct only for the package-level corner-closure route after swap invariance is granted. The paper still leaves open whether the actual corrected two-pair defect package is state-local in the descended quotient-visible transport state, and it allows that any surviving obstruction could be genuinely relational/provenance-sensitive or non-finite-order. with swap invariance already assumed along the involutive branch. task_id: ses_2476b1728ffeKvr2FoYg8TeQTN (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
- Claim  
  Locally on the residual exact fixed-shear corner \(\{S=0\}\cap\{K_v=0\}\), “same reduced image germ at coincidence” is not equivalent to the desired diagonal-vanishing theorem for \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}\). It is a strictly stronger package-level statement. The local diagonal-vanishing target is the first finite-order shadow of that stronger theorem: if the reduced two-atom package collapses on the coincidence diagonal to the one-atom reduced germ, then the cubic and quintic interaction defects vanish on coincident atoms, and together with the involutive swap symmetry this yields \(E_{12}^{(3)}(t),E_{12}^{(5)}(t)=O(t^2)\), hence \(O((h_1-h_2)^2)\). So diagonal vanishing can be viewed as the lowest finite-order slice of the same-reduced-image-germ package, but the full package also carries higher/provenance-sensitive content, notably the order-\(7\) quotient-septic collapse. Therefore the research order should stay as the draft now organizes it: first prove the weaker quotient-diagonal/local package collapse needed to get diagonal vanishing on the residual fixed-shear corner; only then pursue the stronger same-reduced-image-germ theorem if the higher-order coincidence/functoriality lane is still needed.

- Status (`proved | computational | heuristic | open`)  
  heuristic

- Evidence  
  The draft separates the two targets. On the residual fixed-shear corner, Proposition `\ref{prop:residual-involutive-defect-criterion-fixed-shear-corner}` and Corollary `\ref{cor:package-level-reduction-residual-fixed-shear-corner}` show that to exclude the local involutive branch it is enough that the actual cubic and quintic defects are swap-even and vanish on coincident atoms; this gives \(E_{12}^{(3)},E_{12}^{(5)}=O((h_1-h_2)^2)\). By contrast, the “same reduced image germ at coincidence” language is introduced as an honest order-\(7\), provenance-sensitive package theorem strong enough to force quotient-septic collapse after cubic/quintic agreement. The draft explicitly lists these as different downstream programs: first a quotient-diagonal closure theorem on the exact fixed-shear corner, second a stronger provenance-sensitive package theorem such as same reduced image germ or collision-functoriality. That ordering shows the stronger theorem properly contains more information than the diagonal-vanishing theorem. The exact local consequence needed for diagonal vanishing is diagonal package collapse/merger at coincidence: the interaction remainder must vanish when \(h_1=h_2\), so its cubic scalar and quintic fixed-sector projections vanish there. On an involutive branch, evenness under \(t\leftrightarrow -t\) then removes the linear term automatically.

- Strongest objection

So the strict-strength comparison is inferred from how the draft uses the phrase, not from a theorem with hypotheses and conclusion. If the intended definition were weakened to mean only “the reduced package has the same diagonal value at coincidence,” then it would collapse to the diagonal-vanishing input and would no longer be strictly stronger. The current wording in the draft, however, points the other way: it is meant to be strong enough to force quotient-septic collapse, which exceeds cubic/quintic diagonal vanishing.
